ocr_mistral.text:
certify that I have an interest in the prosecution of this claim. That the foregoing declaration and affidavit were read over to, and understood by, the respective parties before they signed the same.

J. P. Breckinridge, Clerk
of the United County Court

NOTE.—If the Clerk of the Court (the Deputy will not answer) takes the Declaration and Affidavit defined of the Judge, he signs the above certificate and places the seal of the Court thereto, and the following certificate then goes unauthenticated, but if the Judge admonitors the oath, he signs the foregoing certificate, and then the Clerk himself authenticated the certificate herein following.

State of
County of
SS.

I, _________________________, Clerk of the _________________________ Court within and for the County and State aforesaid, do hereby certify that _________________________ before whom the foregoing Pension Declaration and joint affidavit were made, and who has thereunto signed his name, was, at the time of so doing, Judge of the _________________________ Court in and for the County and State above named, duly elected, qualified and sworn; that all his official acts as such are entitled to full faith and credit, and that his said signature, as it above appears, is genuine.

Given under my hand and seal of _________________________ Court at office in _________________________ this _________________________ day of _________________________ A. D. 18

Clerk,
Clerk,
Clerk.

NOTE.—The Deputy Clerk of a Court of Record is prohibited by the Rules and Regulations of the Pension Office from taking Pension Declarations, hence the absolute necessity for the Judge or Clerk of the Court to do so personally, and to his official capacity.

FORWARDED BY _________________________ or _________________________

WIDOW'S APPLICATION
FOR
ARMY PENSION.
WAR 1861.

Act of _________________________
Mys. _________________________
Widow of _________________________
Late a _________________________ Co. _________________________
of the _________________________ Regiment of _________________________

PENSION OFFICE CIRCULAR.
JUL 15, 1861.
EXTRACT.
The forms should be exactly followed in every instance. No affidavit will be required as having had the necessary declaration and affidavits as contemplated by the lawsuits, Section of the Act July 14, 1861, under the terms, as well as the instructions given, on strictly complied with.

J. P. B. BARRETT, Commissioner.
Attorney.

INSTRUCTIONS.

1. "Judge," or "Clerk," as the case may be.
2. Name of the Court.
3. "City," "Town," "Deposition," or "County," as the case may be.
4. "July 14, 1861," or any other Act of Congress applicable to the case.
5. "Country, Territory, or Artillery (Chemical or ... State give State), ... naming the troops as indicated according to the arm of the service to which they belong. If of the regular service, in place of the foregoing unit: "United States Infantry," "Carolina," or "Artillery," as the case may be.
6. "State, or," or "State Understood Town," as the case may be.
7. "Branch," or "Registrar," or "No Clerk of Regional Dept." "Pensioner," "Rearor," "Elementary," "Chronic Diarrhea," "Dysentery," "Ciclosa Morbus," "Phillip," or any other disease, physical disability, or injury, whatever it may be, naming it.
8. If killed in Battle, add: "Being killed in Battle of ... date of his death," or if dying of wounds after Battle, instead of the foregoing say: "Received in Battle or Action at ... on the ... day of ... A. D. 18 ...
9. "Home, He ..., about between, ... and ... stands, in the City of ... in the County and State aforesaid," or if, on the other hand, the applicant resides in the country instead of the city, in place of the foregoing say: "on the wagon [or rail] road looking from ... to ... about ... miles from ... the County and of ... County, State above named."
10. "On child law children, time of the above named soldier deceased, who are under the age of sixteen years of the present time, now certain time," or if, on the other hand, he has left any child or children, the same, within the age of sixteen years of this time, the full name, dated (DATE) and residence, of each such child, must be here stated.
11. "Age," or "male," as the case may be.
12. "Name," or "month," as the case may be.

J. P. Breckinridge, Clerk of the United County Court

State of County of SS. NOTE.—The Deputy Clerk of a Court of Record is prohibited by the Rules and Regulations of the Pension Office from taking Pension Declarations, hence the absolute necessity for the Judge or Clerk of the Court to do so personally, and to his official capacity.

J. P. B. BARRETT, Commissioner. Attorney.
